content:
> still no fix from google maps team

That is not correct; see https://issuetracker.google.com/issues/237380999#comment22.

For anyone still seeing this crash, please provide all of:
- The maps renderer you are using. This is the **return value** from `mapsImplementation.initializeWithRenderer`, not just the value you are passing, since not all devices support the latest renderer.
- Your Google Play Services version. This is listed in the details for that application in the apps list in Settings.
- The exception details for the crash, to ensure it's the same underlying issue.

> 

I haven't still published my app. On my real device I get the latest renderer and I get no crash. But on Google Pixel 4 Emulator which supports Google play services I get legacy renderer and app crashes sometimes.
Any updates?

Please do not comment here without reading:
- [this comment](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/105965#issuecomment-1354678187), which explains how to opt into the new Google Maps renderer that fixed this crash a while ago,
- [this comment](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/105965#issuecomment-1503396988), which explains that the code many comments above have (without explanation) suggested adding is almost certainly not a good idea and should not be necessary, and
- [this comment](https://github.com/flutter/flutter/issues/105965#issuecomment-1546876220), which links to information about where the crash was fixed in the old renderer (in Google Play services 22.39.15), and explains how to provide actionable information to determine if there is still anything unresolved here.

Any comments which aren't responsive to those will be hidden as off-topic to keep remaining discussion focused on remaining work, and to avoid this issue being increasingly confusing as to the state and the solutions.

Since the Google Maps team has indicated that this is fixed in both the new and old renderers, the Flutter team's current understanding is that this should now be resolved. Absent concrete evidence (per the third comment listed above) that this isn't the case, this issue will be closed.

The latest renderer has some serious issues it is flickering if we move it constantly and the old renderer crashes the app. 
Should I share a video about what both renderers are doing?

> Should I share a video about what both renderers are doing?

If you can reproduce this crash with the old renderer, please provide the details requested in my comment above.

Anything that's not related to this crash should be filed as a new issue.

- I am using google_maps_flutter: ^2.2.3 

- Flutter 3.10.3 • channel stable • https://github.com/flutter/flutter.git
Framework • revision f92f44110e (7 days ago) • 2023-06-01 18:17:33 -0500
Engine • revision 2a3401c9bb
Tools • Dart 3.0.3 • DevTools 2.23.1


- [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-ZTVX5agLWkYM-N3jjr8y70_Ly3pH6KJ/view?usp=sharing](url)

The above URL video is a legacy URL see how it crashed

- This is the error I am getting while the app crash

 E/gralloc4(12679): Empty SMPTE 2094-40 data

- Google Play service version is - 23.17.15
- I am using Redmi K50i its mid-range phone

- [https://drive.google.com/file/d/1-Sz11lSBdcBNgThLD9xQbD7U9vQ-07Ao/view?usp=sharing](url)

Now this URL video is using the latest renderer and it is flickering


We are a GPS provider company our whole application is dependent upon tracking vehicles and the application is crashing every time.

@stuartmorgan If you need any other detail please tell me

@PritamPathak we experienced same flickering like you with the latest renderer. 

The new renderer not stable. 😏
@PritamPathak if that is the entire crash log, then it's not this issue; it does not have the NPE exception stack that this issue was about. Please file a new issue for that.

> The latest renderer has some serious issues it is flickering if we move it constantly and the old renderer crashes the app.
> Should I share a video about what both renderers are doing?

i was facing same issue and my comment was marked as off-topic 😔

I think you use bottomNavigationBar and google map,
it's not good,
if you use bottomNavigationBar  , you MUST use IndexedStack,
then Everything OK,

> I think you use bottomNavigationBar and google map,
> it's not good,
> if you use bottomNavigationBar , you MUST use IndexedStack,
> then Everything OK,

Thanks for your suggestion but it doesn't resolve the issue I tried IndexedStack @panko4545

I've heard back from the Google Maps team that they are no longer fixing bugs in GMM6 (aka LEGACY) renderer. The crashes in this bug all seem related to GMM6/Legacy renderer and are not in code that the Flutter team owns. Therefore, I am closing this bug as we have no meaningful way of addressing it. I highly encourage you to move off of the LEGACY renderer and if you encounter bugs using the new renderer, please file them and we can (hopefully) address them.

> I've heard back from the Google Maps team that they are no longer fixing bugs in GMM6 (aka LEGACY) renderer. The crashes in this bug all seem related to GMM6/Legacy renderer and are not in code that the Flutter team owns. Therefore, I am closing this bug as we have no meaningful way of addressing it. I highly encourage you to move off of the LEGACY renderer and if you encounter bugs using the new renderer, please file them and we can (hopefully) address them.

## Add PopupMenuTheme to enable theming color, shape, elevation, text style of Menu ## Description This change introduces a PopupMenuEntryTheme that allows you to theme the color, shape, elevation, and text style of a menu. This can be done at the theme level and at call time of showMenu. See the following image for an example: A gist showing how to generate this menu is [here](https://gist.github.com/lisa-liao/abcd2b51ed688b8221edf17d85eca958). ## Related Issues Closes #36076 ## Tests I added the following tests: *Tests for the theme and modifying the color/shape/elevation/text style of a menu ## Checklist Before you create this PR confirm that it meets all requirements listed below by checking the relevant checkboxes (`[x]`). ## Implement Regular Windows for the win32 framework + add an example application for regular windows

## What's new?
- Implement and test `_window_win32.dart`, the Win32 implementation of `_window.dart` :tada: 
- Wrote a bunch of tests in `window_win32_test.dart` which uses a mock API barrier to mock the native Win32 layer
- Update `BaseWindowController` to extend `ChangeNotifier` and use it to inform the `WindowScope` of when things change
- Made `RegularWindowController.empty()` a non-internal constructor, as it is needed by implementations
- Added a _large_ example application for windowing where users can test out different windowing concepts and see how they work!

To test, simply run the tests or try out the example app!

## Pre-launch Checklist

- [x] I read the [Contributor Guide] and followed the process outlined there for submitting PRs.
- [x] I read the [Tree Hygiene] wiki page, which explains my responsibilities.
- [x] I read and followed the [Flutter Style Guide], including [Features we expect every widget to implement].
- [x] I signed the [CLA].
- [x] I listed at least one issue that this PR fixes in the description above.
- [x] I updated/added relevant documentation (doc comments with `///`).
- [x] I added new tests to check the change I am making, or this PR is [test-exempt].
- [x] I followed the [breaking change policy] and added [Data Driven Fixes] where supported.
- [x] All existing and new tests are passing.

##  Observatory port timeout on iOS

I'm unable to debug a freshly created app on physical iOS devices when pubspec dependencies reach a certain size, because it causes the "waiting for observatory port" step to timeout after 30 seconds.

Devices tested:
iPhone 8
iPhone 12
iPhone 12 Pro

iOS versions tested:
14.2
14.2.1
14.3

Xcode versions tested:
12.2
12.3

Tests run using MacOS Catalina 10.15.7 on a late 2019 MBP 16".

Simply commenting out random pubspec dependencies from the below, cleaning and rebuilding allows debugging to attach - sometimes just barely, at the 28 second or 29 second mark.

Here's a triggering pubspec:

```
dependencies:
  flutter:
    sdk: flutter
  flutter_localizations:
    sdk: flutter
  http: ^0.12.2
  in_app_review: ^1.0.2
  local_auth: ^0.6.3
  location: ^3.0.2
  logger: ^0.9.4
  modal_bottom_sheet: ^1.0.0+1
  moor: ^3.4.0
  moor_db_viewer: ^2.0.3
  moor_flutter: ^3.1.0
  overlay_support: ^1.0.5
  package_info: ^0.4.3+2
  path: ^1.7.0
  path_provider: ^1.6.24
  pinput: ^0.2.0
  purchases_flutter: ^1.4.3
  recase: ^3.0.0
  rxdart: ^0.24.1
  shared_preferences: ^0.5.8
  sqlite3_flutter_libs: ^0.2.0
  syncfusion_flutter_charts: 18.2.59
  timeago: ^2.0.28
  universal_platform: ^0.1.3
  url_launcher: ^5.7.8
  vibrate: ^0.0.4
  video_player: ^1.0.0
  webview_flutter: ^1.0.7
```
